failed_cases.description: Block quotes expose nested block children through the public tree.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
quote = Markdown('ast').parse('> # Quote\n> text')[0]
print(quote['type'])
print([(child['type'], child.get('level'), child.get('text')) for child in quote['children']])

failed_cases.description: Malformed inline constructs remain literal and unknown plugin names raise ValueError.
Table recognition is a block-boundary lifecycle decision: valid header/delimiter order creates one table, but paragraph-embedded or reordered pipe lines do not.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown

def types(src):
    return [t.get('type') for t in Markdown('ast', plugins=['table'])(src) if isinstance(t, dict)]

def table(src):
    ast = Markdown('ast', plugins=['table'])(src)
    return next((t for t in ast if isinstance(t, dict) and t.get('type') == 'table'), None)

valid = '''| A | B |
| --- | --- |
| *x* | **y** |'''
embedded = '''Intro paragraph
A | B
| --- | --- |'''
reordered = '''| --- | --- |
| A | B |
| 1 | 2 |'''
t = table(valid)
print(types(valid) == ['table'], 'table' not in types(embedded), 'table' not in types(reordered))
print(t is not None and len(t.get('header', [])) == 2 and len(t.get('rows', [])) == 1)

failed_cases.description: Tight and loose list item state is represented in the canonical tree while inline children remain renderable through replay.
failed_cases.expected_output:
True True True True
True True True
failed_cases.actual:
True True True False
False False False
failed_cases.expected:
True True True True
True True True

all_cases.description: Public imports, callable parser, canonical-tree helper methods, renderer construction, and escaping are exposed through the documented API.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown, HTMLRenderer, ASTRenderer, escape_html
m = Markdown()
print(callable(m), all(hasattr(m, name) for name in ['parse', 'tokens', 'walk', 'render', 'toc', 'register_inline', 'register_block']))
print(Markdown(renderer=HTMLRenderer())('plain'))
print(Markdown(renderer='ast')('plain')[0]['type'])
print(escape_html('"<&>'))

all_cases.description: An isolated ATX heading exposes level, source text, inline children, and generated id on the public token.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast').parse('# Alpha & Beta!')[0]
print(t['type'], t.get('level'), t.get('text'), t.get('attrs', {}).get('id'))
print([c['type'] for c in t.get('children', [])])

all_cases.description: Fenced code blocks expose public text and optional language fields.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast').parse('```python\nprint("<x>")\n```')[0]
print(t['type'], t.get('lang'), t.get('text'))

all_cases.description: Indented code and thematic breaks are recognized as block nodes without inline parsing.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast').parse('    a < b\n    c\n\n---')
print(tokens[0]['type'], tokens[0]['text'].startswith('a < b\nc'))
print(tokens[1]['type'])

all_cases.description: Simple ordered and unordered lists expose ordered state and item text for one-node list contracts.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast').parse('- one
- two

1. first
2. second')
print([(t['ordered'], [i.get('text') for i in t['items']]) for t in tokens])

all_cases.description: Block quotes expose nested block children through the public tree.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
quote = Markdown('ast').parse('> # Quote\n> text')[0]
print(quote['type'])
print([(child['type'], child.get('level'), child.get('text')) for child in quote['children']])

all_cases.description: Emphasis and strong emphasis parse as inline containers with child text.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
children = Markdown('ast')('*em* and __strong__')[0]['children']
print([c['type'] for c in children])
print(children[0]['children'][0]['text'], children[2]['children'][0]['text'])

all_cases.description: Inline code spans are literal islands in HTML rendering.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
print(Markdown()('Use `**literal** <tag>` now'))

all_cases.description: Links and images expose nested label/alt parsing and escaped attributes through rendering.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('[*docs*](https://e.test?a=1&b=2 "T & Q") ![A *B*](img.png "Pic")') print('docs' in html, 'href="https://e.test?a=1&b=2"' in html, 'title="T & Q"' in html) print('' in html)

all_cases.description: Escapes, autolinks, and public line-break tokens work for isolated inline content.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
m = Markdown('ast')
children = m('Escaped \\* star and <https://example.test>  \nnext\\\nline')[0]['children']
print(any(c.get('type') == 'link' and c.get('url') == 'https://example.test' for c in children))
print([c.get('type') for c in children].count('line_break'))
print('Escaped * star' in Markdown()('Escaped \\* star'))

all_cases.description: HTML rendering escapes literal text and attributes from direct public token dictionaries.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import HTMLRenderer, escape_html
print(escape_html('"<&>'))
html = HTMLRenderer().render([{'type':'paragraph','children':[{'type':'link','url':'https://e.test?a=1&b="q"','title':'A & B','children':[{'type':'text','text':'go'}]}]}])
print(html)

all_cases.description: HTML renderer emits core block tags, including heading anchors, from direct public token dictionaries.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import HTMLRenderer
tokens = [{'type':'heading','level':2,'attrs':{'id':'hi-there'},'children':[{'type':'text','text':'Hi'}]},{'type':'thematic_break'},{'type':'block_code','text':'x < y','lang':'py'}]
print(HTMLRenderer().render(tokens))

all_cases.description: AST rendering returns clean public dictionaries with nested inline children and no private state.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast')('Text **bold** and `code`')
para = tokens[0]
print(type(tokens).__name__, para['type'])
print([c['type'] for c in para['children']])
print(any(k.startswith('_') for t in tokens for k in t.keys()))

all_cases.description: The strikethrough plugin registers inline syntax and local HTML rendering.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
print(Markdown(plugins=['strikethrough'])('~~gone & back~~'))

all_cases.description: The table plugin recognizes the pipe table block shape and alignments.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast', plugins=['table'])('| A | B | C |\n| :--- | :---: | ---: |\n| 1 | 2 | 3 |')[0]
print(t['type'], t['align'])
print([c['text'] for c in t['header']])
print([c['text'] for c in t['rows'][0]])

all_cases.description: The task-list plugin records checked state while preserving item text.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
items = Markdown('ast', plugins=['task_list'])('- [x] done\n- [ ] todo')[0]['items']
print([(i.get('checked'), i.get('text')) for i in items])

all_cases.description: Custom inline plugin callables can register public parse and render handlers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown

def mark_plugin(md):
    md.register_inline('mark', r'==([^=]+)==', lambda m: {'text': m.group(1)}, lambda r, t: '<mark>' + r.render_inlines([{'type':'text','text':t['text']}]) + '</mark>')

print(Markdown(plugins=[mark_plugin])('A ==hit & run=='))

all_cases.description: Malformed inline constructs remain literal and unknown plugin names raise ValueError.
parse(), tokens(), AST rendering, and walk() expose the same canonical tree for a mixed plugin document.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown

def has_type(md, tree, kind):
    return any(isinstance(n, dict) and n.get('type') == kind for n in md.walk(tree))

doc = '''# API **SDK**

Para ~~*gone*~~.

- [x] ship
- [ ] wait

| A | B |
| --- | --- |
| `x` | [y](https://e.test) |'''
m = Markdown('ast', plugins=['strikethrough', 'table', 'task_list'])
tree = m.parse(doc)
print(tree == m.tokens(doc) == m(doc))
print(all(has_type(m, tree, kind) for kind in ['heading', 'paragraph', 'list', 'table', 'strikethrough', 'code_span', 'link']))
print([n.get('checked') for n in m.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, dict) and 'checked' in n] == [True, False])

all_cases.description: Rendering an existing parse tree through HTML and AST replay is deterministic and does not mutate the tree.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
from html.parser import HTMLParser
import copy

class Tags(HTMLParser):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.tags = []
    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        self.tags.append(tag)

doc = 'Para ~~*gone*~~.\n\n| A | B |\n| --- | --- |\n| `x` | **y** |'
m = Markdown(plugins=['strikethrough', 'table'])
tree = m.parse(doc)
snapshot = copy.deepcopy(tree)
html1 = m.render(tree)
html2 = m.render(tree)
ast_replay = m.render(tree, renderer='ast')
p = Tags(); p.feed(html1)
print(tree == snapshot, html1 == html2, ast_replay == snapshot)
print(all(tag in p.tags for tag in ['p', 'del', 'em', 'table', 'code', 'strong']))

all_cases.description: Heading token ids, TOC entries, and rendered HTML heading ids/text are derived from one inline-interpreted heading projection.
Block quotes, lists, and table cells all contribute nested inline nodes to the same walked tree and equivalent rendered tags.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
from html.parser import HTMLParser

class Tags(HTMLParser):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__(); self.tags = []
    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        self.tags.append(tag)

def count_type(md, tree, kind):
    return sum(1 for n in md.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, dict) and n.get('type') == kind)

doc = '''> ## Inner *Head*
> quoted **text**
>
> - item [one](https://e.test)

| A | B |
| --- | --- |
| `x` | *y* |'''
m = Markdown(plugins=['table'])
tree = m.parse(doc)
html = m.render(tree)
p = Tags(); p.feed(html)
print(count_type(m, tree, 'emphasis') >= 2, count_type(m, tree, 'strong') == 1, count_type(m, tree, 'link') == 1, count_type(m, tree, 'code_span') == 1)
print(all(tag in p.tags for tag in ['blockquote', 'h2', 'ul', 'li', 'a', 'table', 'code']))

all_cases.description: A custom inline plugin token keeps metadata consistently across parse, walk, AST replay, and HTML replay.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown, escape_html def badge_plugin(md): md.register_inline('badge', r'\[\[([^\]]+)\]\]', lambda m: {'text': m.group(1), 'meta': {'plugin': 'badge', 'length': len(m.group(1))}}, lambda r, t: '%s' % (escape_html(t['meta']['plugin']), escape_html(t['text']))) def badges(md, tree): return [n for n in md.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, dict) and n.get('type') == 'badge'] m = Markdown(plugins=[badge_plugin]) tree = m.parse('Ship [[v1 & beta]] then [[ok]]') ast = m.render(tree, renderer='ast') html = m.render(tree) print(badges(m, tree) == badges(m, ast)) print([b['meta']['length'] for b in badges(m, tree)] == [9, 2]) print(html.count('data-plugin="badge"') == 2, 'v1 & beta' in html)

all_cases.description: A tree produced through the AST renderer can be replayed by an HTML renderer with the same core/plugin semantics as direct HTML rendering.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown, HTMLRenderer doc = '''# H - [x] ~~*one*~~ - [ ] `two` | A | B | | --- | --- | | **x** | [y](https://e.test) |''' plugins = ['strikethrough', 'task_list', 'table'] ast_tree = Markdown('ast', plugins=plugins)(doc) html_from_ast_tree = HTMLRenderer().render(ast_tree) html_direct = Markdown(plugins=plugins)(doc) print(html_from_ast_tree == html_direct) print(all(fragment in html_from_ast_tree for fragment in ['one', '', '', 'y']))

all_cases.description: Parsing, rendering, TOC projection, and parsing again on one parser preserve semantic tree and derived ids.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown

doc = '''# Repeat

Para ~~gone~~.

## Repeat

| A | B |
| --- | --- |
| *x* | `y` |'''
m = Markdown(plugins=['strikethrough', 'table'])
tree1 = m.parse(doc)
html1 = m.render(tree1)
toc1 = m.toc(doc)
tree2 = m.parse(doc)
html2 = m(doc)
toc2 = m.toc(doc)
print(tree1 == tree2, html1 == html2, toc1 == toc2)
print([e['id'] for e in toc1] == ['repeat', 'repeat-2'])

all_cases.description: Malformed inline/plugin syntax remains local literal text and does not poison later canonical parses on the same parser.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown m = Markdown(plugins=['strikethrough']) bad = 'Bad ~~strike and [link](oops then **ok**' good = 'Next ~~good~~ and *fine*' bad_html = m(bad) tree1 = m.parse(good) html1 = m.render(tree1) tree2 = m.parse(good) html2 = m(good) print('~~strike' in bad_html, 'ok' in bad_html) print(tree1 == tree2, html1 == html2) print(any(n.get('type') == 'strikethrough' for n in m.walk(tree1) if isinstance(n, dict)), any(n.get('type') == 'emphasis' for n in m.walk(tree1) if isinstance(n, dict)))
all_cases.expected_output:
True True
True True
True True
all_cases.actual:
True True
True True
True True
all_cases.expected:
True True
True True
True True

all_cases.description: Table recognition is a block-boundary lifecycle decision: valid header/delimiter order creates one table, but paragraph-embedded or reordered pipe lines do not.

from minimarkdown import Markdown

def types(src):
    return [t.get('type') for t in Markdown('ast', plugins=['table'])(src) if isinstance(t, dict)]

def table(src):
    ast = Markdown('ast', plugins=['table'])(src)
    return next((t for t in ast if isinstance(t, dict) and t.get('type') == 'table'), None)

valid = '''| A | B |
| --- | --- |
| *x* | **y** |'''
embedded = '''Intro paragraph
A | B
| --- | --- |'''
reordered = '''| --- | --- |
| A | B |

t = table(valid)
print(types(valid) == ['table'], 'table' not in types(embedded), 'table' not in types(reordered))
print(t is not None and len(t.get('header', [])) == 2 and len(t.get('rows', [])) == 1)
